action.reasoning: Observation: Current best test PESQ=3.137 with fixed loudness=-25 (val PESQ=2.862 on 100 samples); matching noisy loudness and wide range of fixed targets (-35 to -23) all yield identical val PESQ (flat curve). MetricGAN+ pretrained is strong task-specific baseline, no training done yet. test_enhance.py works but has truncation artifact at end. Analysis: Iterative application of enhancer (2 passes) may further suppress residual noise and improve perceptual quality (common heuristic in SE); combining with loudness norm could help. Training ConvTasNet from scratch unlikely to beat 3.137 in few epochs given literature numbers and time per epoch (~5min). Decision: Rewrite test_enhance.py cleanly (load best_target, test 4 strategies on 100 val samples: fixed, matching, iterative-fixed, iterative-matching), measure PESQ+time. Pick best strategy, update predict.py accordingly, then submit_eval on metricgan_checkpoint.
